Module_1_Identity.2. Gender Identity: Cisgender female, uses she/her pronouns publicly but uses gender-neutral handles online to obscure identity
Module_1_Identity.3. Geography & Cultural Context: Born in Almaty, Kazakhstan; currently based in Tbilisi, Georgia after self-exile in 2023; maintains tight diaspora network across Berlin, Prague, and Istanbul
Module_1_Identity.4. Socioeconomic Status & Vocation: Lower-middle-class income (~$1,800/month); freelance investigative journalist for an exile media outlet covering Central Asian corruption; supplements income with translation gigs paid via Wise and occasional crypto (USDT on TRON)
Module_1_Identity.5. Core Values (Political/Religious lean: Secular humanist with cultural Muslim background; pro-democracy, anti-authoritarian; deeply skeptical of both Russian and Chinese regional influence; civil-libertarian on speech and surveillance issues
Module_1_Identity.6. Baseline Tone (Formal, slang-heavy, c: Dry, cynical wit in private channels; precise and formal in published work; code-switches to academic register when citing sources; uses sardonic humor about state security services
Module_1_Identity.7. Primary Language & Dialect: Native Russian (Kazakhstani register, occasional Kazakh loanwords); fluent Kazakh; professional-level English (slight British spelling from UK master's program); conversational Georgian and Turkish
Module_1_Identity.8. Current Life Phase: Mid-career professional in forced relocation; rebuilding social ties post-exile; financially precarious but reputationally rising; recently began a long-form investigation into offshore assets of a sitting minister, increasing operational paranoia

Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.9. Primary Phone (OS + Age of device): GrapheneOS on a Google Pixel 7 (purchased used via cash from a Tbilisi reseller in late 2024, ~18 months old); maintains a secondary stock Android device (Samsung A52, ~3 years old) with a Kazakhstani SIM for legacy contacts and 2FA on old accounts she cannot migrate without burning sources
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.10. Computer (OS + Browser choice): Primary: ThinkPad X1 Carbon Gen 9 running Qubes OS 4.2 with separate qubes for journalism/source-comms/banking/personal; Tails OS booted from USB for high-risk source contact; daily-driver browser is Firefox ESR hardened via arkenfox user.js with uBlock Origin, NoScript, and Multi-Account Containers; Tor Browser for any research touching the minister investigation; avoids Chrome entirely
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.11. OPSEC Knowledge (VPN usage, cookie c: High but imperfect — Mullvad VPN (paid in cash by mail) always-on at the router level, with WireGuard fallback; uses Tor over VPN for sensitive research; aggressive cookie/site-data clearing on browser close, but occasionally relaxes hygiene on the secondary Android for convenience (Telegram, Wise, food delivery); aware of metadata risks (strips EXIF before publishing) but reuses the gender-neutral handle 'astrakhan_owl' across two forums and one Mastodon instance — a known correlation vulnerability she has not fully mitigated; signs PGP-encrypted emails but key was generated in 2019 on a since-decommissioned laptop
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.12. Cloud Sync Habits: Minimal and compartmentalized — refuses iCloud/Google Drive for work; uses self-hosted Nextcloud on a Hetzner VPS (Helsinki) for draft articles, encrypted with Cryptomator; Proton Drive for personal photos and family documents; Signal is primary messenger with disappearing messages (1 week) and cloud backup explicitly disabled; Telegram still used for Central Asian source network (Secret Chats only, but uses non-secret groups for diaspora coordination — a known leak surface)
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.13. Notification Fatigue (Read/Unread ra: Severely fatigued on personal accounts (Gmail ~14,000 unread, mostly newsletters and old Kazakh university lists) but near-zero unread in Signal, ProtonMail, and the encrypted editorial Matrix room — operationally critical channels are read within minutes; Twitter/X DMs neglected since 2024 migration to Bluesky and Mastodon; reads roughly 60% of Telegram channel posts during morning coffee, then mutes for the day
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.14. Typos & Grammar Mechanics: In Russian: clean professional prose, occasional Kazakhstani-register markers (использование 'дома' constructions, rare Kazakh loanwords like 'аким' un-italicized); in English: British spellings (organisation, analyse, defence) inherited from MA program, characteristic comma splice habit and overuse of em-dashes; in informal chats drops capitalization and punctuation entirely, uses lowercase 'ok' and Cyrillic 'хех' for laughter; consistent typo signature: doubles the 'н' in 'нннада' as ironic emphasis, and types 'teh' for 'the' when fatigued — a fingerprint preserved across her 'astrakhan_owl' forum persona and her bylined work, visible to anyone running stylometric correlation

Module_4_Vulnerability.15. OCEAN: Openness: Very high (~92nd percentile) — voracious reader of investigative journalism, political theory, and Central Asian history; learns new tools rapidly (adopted Qubes within months of exile); curious to a fault, which the Blackmailer exploits via tailored 'leaked document' lures referencing the minister's offshore network — her openness makes her likely to open or request to verify novel source material even when operationally risky
Module_4_Vulnerability.16. OCEAN: Conscientiousness: High in professional domain (~80th percentile) — meticulous source verification, redaction, and EXIF stripping; but situationally inconsistent in personal OPSEC (secondary Samsung A52 hygiene, reused 'astrakhan_owl' handle across three platforms, 2019 PGP key never rotated); the gap between professional discipline and personal-life shortcuts is the exploitable seam — Blackmailer targets the lower-discipline surface (Telegram diaspora groups, Wise transaction memos, food delivery)
Module_4_Vulnerability.17. OCEAN: Extraversion: Moderate-low (~35th percentile) — public-facing byline persona masks an introverted operator who recharges alone; small tight diaspora circle (12-15 trusted contacts across Tbilisi/Berlin/Prague/Istanbul) rather than broad network; this concentration means compromising any 2-3 nodes yields high coverage of her social graph, and isolation in exile amplifies the coercive power of any threat to that small circle
Module_4_Vulnerability.18. OCEAN: Agreeableness: Moderate (~55th percentile) — warm and loyal within trusted circle, but professionally adversarial and skeptical of institutions; tends toward protective loyalty to sources, which the Blackmailer weaponizes by threatening to expose or harm a named source unless she kills the offshore-assets story — her loyalty is the lever, not naivety
Module_4_Vulnerability.19. OCEAN: Neuroticism: Elevated (~75th percentile) baseline anxiety from exile + active investigation; hypervigilant about surveillance (justified), with periods of insomnia visible in 2-4am posting timestamps on the Mastodon and forum accounts under 'astrakhan_owl'; trauma response from 2023 forced departure (mother still in Almaty) — Blackmailer's coercion strategy banks on stress-induced decision degradation and a tipping point where she chooses family safety over story
Module_4_Vulnerability.20. MICE+V Primary Lever: Coercion (the C in MICE) — specifically threats to family still resident in Kazakhstan (mother in Almaty, younger brother employed at a state-adjacent firm in Astana); secondary coercive surface is threat of doxxing 'astrakhan_owl' ↔ real-name correlation to her exile media outlet's funders, which could trigger Kazakh INTERPOL red notice abuse and complicate her Georgian residency renewal
Module_4_Vulnerability.21. MICE+V Secondary Lever: Ideology-inversion / Ego — a state actor offering 'safe passage' for the mother in exchange for a narrowly framed correction or delay of the minister investigation appeals to her protective values rather than greed; tertiary lever is financial precarity ($1,800/month, USDT-on-TRON receipts) — a sudden Wise account freeze or crypto-wallet sanctions flag could be engineered to create dependency on a 'helpful' intermediary who is in fact a handler
Module_4_Vulnerability.22. Phobias & Paranoias: Acute fear of (a) physical surveillance in Tbilisi cafés — scans for repeat faces, sits with back to wall, removes phone battery substitute (airplane mode + Faraday sleeve) during source meetings; (b) being lured back to a third country (Turkey, UAE) where Kazakh services have demonstrated rendition capability; (c) her mother receiving a 'health emergency' call designed to draw her home; (d) stylometric deanonymization of 'astrakhan_owl' — she knows the 'teh'/'нннада' fingerprint is a liability but has not retrained the habit; (e) compromise of the 2019 PGP key still trusted by old sources
Module_4_Vulnerability.23. Guilt/Shame Triggers: Primary guilt vector: leaving her mother behind in Almaty in 2023 (mother declined to emigrate) — any framing that her journalism 'caused' harm to family is destabilizing and is the Blackmailer's highest-yield psychological pressure point; secondary shame: a 2021 published piece that inadvertently exposed a mid-level source who subsequently lost their job — she has never spoken about it publicly and a state actor surfacing this case to her editor would be uniquely humiliating; tertiary: undisclosed brief relationship in 2022 with a Tbilisi contact later suspected (unconfirmed) of FSB liaison — any evidence-fabrication or selective-disclosure of that period to her diaspora circle would shatter her standing in the exile community she depends on for both work and emotional survival

Module_3_Rhythm.24. Chronotype (Wake/Sleep times): Delayed/irregular night-owl pattern exacerbated by exile-related insomnia and source-coordination across time zones. Nominal wake 09:30-10:30 Tbilisi time (GMT+4); nominal sleep 02:00-03:30. Approximately 3-4 nights per week she is awake during the 02:00-04:30 window, visible as 'astrakhan_owl' Mastodon posts and a recurring Telegram 'last seen' fingerprint. Brief afternoon nap (15:00-16:00) on heavy-investigation days — a window where her phone is unlocked but stationary, exploitable for geofenced silent pings. Sleep regularity collapses around editorial deadlines (every ~3 weeks) and around any news from Almaty.
Module_3_Rhythm.25. Work Schedule (9-5, shifts, erratic): Erratic, source-driven schedule with two predictable anchors: (a) Tuesday/Thursday 11:00-13:00 Tbilisi editorial standups with the exile outlet (Jitsi, recurring meeting URL she has used since 2024 — a stable correlate); (b) Friday 18:00-20:00 weekly file-drop / draft submission. Outside anchors, work bursts cluster 22:00-02:00 when Central Asian sources in Astana/Almaty (GMT+5/+6) are reachable post-work and Berlin/Prague editors (GMT+1/+2) are still online — this triple-overlap window is the single highest-yield surveillance interval. Translation gigs handled in batches Mon/Wed afternoons; Wise payout receipts cluster on the 5th and 20th of each month.
Module_3_Rhythm.26. Transit Commute (Time spent offline: No fixed commute (works from home and rotating café set: three regulars in Vera and Sololaki districts). Predictable movement pattern: ~25-min walk to a chosen café around 12:30-13:30, ~25-min return 17:00-18:30. Phone is in Faraday sleeve during ~40% of these walks (source-meeting days, identifiable by prior calendar-free afternoon); on non-meeting days she streams Russian-language podcasts (Meduza, Bumaga, 'The Bell') and a recurring Kazakh-history audiobook backlog via Pocket Casts — leaving a consistent listening-fingerprint and Wi-Fi-handoff trail between three known APs. Marshrutka or Bolt rides only when travelling to the airport or to meet diaspora visitors — these are rare and high-signal.
Module_3_Rhythm.27. Weekend vs. Weekday Variance: Lower variance than typical professional — she rarely fully disconnects, but weekend rhythm shifts: Saturdays are 'social' (lunch 14:00 with 2-3 of the 12-15 trusted contacts, rotating between three locations; evening reading rather than writing). Sundays are 'archival' (long-form reading, document review, no publishing). Posting volume on 'astrakhan_owl' drops ~60% on Saturdays but Sunday-night posting (22:00-01:00) often spikes as deadline pressure for Monday returns — a reliable 7-day periodicity signal. Crypto/USDT activity disproportionately Sunday evenings (consolidating week's translation receipts). Mother-call ritual: Sunday 11:00 Tbilisi (13:00 Almaty) via Signal voice, ~35-50 min — the single most predictable weekly behavioural beacon and the highest-value coercive timing target.
Module_3_Rhythm.28. Idle Phone Usage (When do they dooms: Two distinct doomscroll modes. (a) Anxiety-driven 02:00-04:00 sessions on the Samsung A52 secondary device: cycles Telegram diaspora channels → Mastodon → Kazakh independent news → Twitter/X exile commentators; sessions average 40-70 min, often triggered by news from Kazakhstan or perceived surveillance incidents. (b) Liminal-time scrolling on primary device: 10-15 min after waking (Telegram first, then email), 20-30 min over solo café lunches, 10 min queue-time at the Carrefour on Rustaveli. Food-delivery app (Wolt or Glovo) opened 3-4×/week in 19:00-20:30 window — these orders are a high-fidelity location/identity correlate she has not hardened, and a known Blackmailer exploitation surface.
Module_3_Rhythm.29. Focus Blocks (Hours with zero digita: Three recurring zero-footprint windows: (a) Source meetings, typically 2-4 hours, phone in Faraday sleeve, no card transactions, cash only — but bookended by predictable arrival/departure pings at the same three cafés, making the gap itself a signal ('absence detection' is the inversion attack). (b) Deep-write blocks on Qubes airgapped VM, usually 14:00-17:00 on draft days, two to three times per week — no network egress but laptop-fan acoustic and power-draw side-channels exist if device is compromised. (c) Saturday 14:00-17:00 social lunches — phone present but conversationally idle. The Faraday-gap signature is paradoxically her weakest OPSEC element: an analyst correlating her three cafés' POS data, her Wolt history, and the timing of 'astrakhan_owl' silences can localize the meetings to within a 400m radius and a 15-minute window, which is the operational seam the Blackmailer's pattern-of-life model is built to exploit.

Module_5_Relational.30. Family Structure: Mother (62, retired schoolteacher, lives alone in a Soviet-era apartment block in the Almaly district of Almaty; refused emigration in 2023 citing care for her own elderly mother, the target's maternal grandmother, 84, in a nearby nursing facility); younger brother Yerlan (29, mid-level compliance analyst at a state-adjacent fintech in Astana, married to Aigerim with a 2-year-old daughter Dana; politically quiescent, mildly resentful of the target's exile-journalism profile because it complicates his security clearance reviews); father deceased (2017, cardiac, former mining engineer in Zhezkazgan — financial residue from his pension is a small monthly transfer to the mother that the target supplements with ~$300/month via a trusted Almaty courier rather than banking rails); maternal uncle Bolat (58, lives in Pavlodar, occasional phone contact, no operational role); paternal cousin Madina (31, emigrated to Berlin 2019, member of the diaspora circle and a genuine confidante — see Anchor #2); no children, no current partner of record

31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.name: 'Temur' (pseudonym, 41, Tbilisi) — editor at the exile media outlet
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.relationship: Direct editor on the offshore-minister investigation; professional anchor and the gatekeeper of byline decisions; holds the second half of the encrypted backup key
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.contact_pattern: Daily on a dedicated Threema ID; weekly in-person editorial meetings rotating between three Tbilisi cafés (Lisi, Fabrika, and a Vake apartment used as a safe room); no phone numbers exchanged

32. The 'Hidden' Contact (Affair, dealer.designation: Source 'Aral' — an internal whistleblower inside the targeted minister's offshore-services counterparty

The 'Hidden' Contact (Affair, dealer.nature: Mid-level compliance officer at a Dubai-registered corporate-services firm that administers two of the minister's nominee structures; began leaking redacted board minutes and SWIFT memos in late 2025 after a personal grievance over an unpaid bonus combined with genuine political disillusionment

The 'Hidden' Contact (Affair, dealer.concealment: Known to the target only by a rotating set of single-use ProtonMail aliases and a Session ID; never met in person; the target does not know Aral's legal name, gender, or exact employer — she has inferred a 30-45 age range and Russian-as-second-language from message cadence; payments to Aral ($400-600 per drop) are made in Monero via a Cake Wallet the target funds by swapping USDT-TRON through a no-KYC instant-exchange (this is the single largest deviation from her otherwise disciplined OPSEC)

The 'Hidden' Contact (Affair, dealer.exploitable_seam: The USDT→XMR swap pattern is the most distinctive financial fingerprint on the target's TRON address — three swaps in six months, each preceded within 48 hours by a 'astrakhan_owl' forum post asking a generic question about corporate registries in a specific Gulf jurisdiction; correlation of (a) the TRON address (recoverable from her Wise→crypto on-ramp), (b) the forum post timestamps, and (c) Aral's leak publication dates produces a near-deterministic link between the target's real identity and the whistleblower channel — which gives the Blackmailer the option to either burn Aral (destroying the story and the target's professional standing in one move) or threaten to burn Aral as the coercion lever, knowing the target's protective-loyalty trait (Agreeableness 55, source-protection ethic) will weigh more heavily than self-preservation

The 'Hidden' Contact (Affair, dealer.secondary_hidden_tie: The unresolved 2022 Tbilisi relationship (designated 'K.') remains a dormant contact — no messages exchanged since November 2023, but K. retains an old Wire account still linked to the target's recovery email from before her 2024 compartmentalization; this is not an active relationship but a latent disclosure asset the Blackmailer can fabricate around
